pr.title: Use custom container for GitHub Actions
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Proposed moving the GitHub Actions build and publish jobs onto a prebuilt custom Docker container, `ribbanya/melee:20230219`, containing an upgraded Debian/devkitPro base, WiBo, patched MWCC tools, Python 3.11 virtualenv dependencies, and documentation tooling. The goal was to remove repeated CI setup work from workflows and simplify build/publish steps. The PR was closed unmerged and explicitly marked as superseded by #751.

key_files.change: Added a multi-stage container build based on devkitpro/devkitppc:20230110, upgrading through Debian bullseye to bookworm, installing build packages, building WiBo, downloading and patching GC/Wii compilers, installing MWCC under /usr/local/bin, and creating a Python venv.

Replaced per-run devkitPro/i386 package installation, WiBo checkout/build, and compiler download/patch steps with use of `container: ribbanya/melee:20230219`; build step now symlinks `/usr/local/bin/mwcc_compiler` into tools and uses `WINE=$WINE`.

Added the same custom container to the GitHub Pages documentation job, removed apt and setup-python steps, and consolidated Doxygen/nojekyll/context generation around the container-provided environment.

what_changed: Added a Dockerfile defining a reusable CI image for Melee builds and documentation publishing.
Upgraded the devkitPro container base from an older Debian release path toward Debian testing/bookworm to support Python 3.11.
Moved WiBo cloning, CMake build, executable installation, and `WINE=/usr/local/bin/wibo` setup into the container.
Moved GC/Wii compiler download, `mwld_patch.py` patching, SHA1 validation, and MWCC installation into the container instead of doing it during every CI build.
Created `/usr/local/bin/mwcceppc` as a shell wrapper invoking the MWCC executable through `$WINE`.
Created a Python virtualenv at `/usr/local/share/venv` and installed project requirements during container build.
Simplified `.github/workflows/build.yml` by deleting repeated dependency, WiBo, and compiler setup steps.
Updated the build artifact upload action from `actions/upload-artifact@v2` to `actions/upload-artifact@v3`.
Simplified `.github/workflows/publish.yml` by relying on container-provided Doxygen/Python tooling and combining related shell commands.
Closed without merge because the proposal was superseded by PR #751.
smart_moves: Prebaking expensive and fragile setup steps into a container would reduce CI runtime variance: WiBo compilation, compiler download, compiler patching, apt package installation, and Python setup would no longer repeat on every run.
Keeping the workflows small made the build intent clearer: checkout, build, upload map for build CI; checkout, generate docs/context, upload/deploy for publish CI.
The Dockerfile preserved toolchain integrity checks by retaining `sha1sum -c tools/mwld_prepatch.sha1` before patching and `sha1sum -c tools/mwld_postpatch.sha1` afterward.
Setting `WINE` as an environment variable in the image let the workflow call `make ... WINE=$WINE` instead of hardcoding a repository-local WiBo build path.
Adding the MWCC toolchain under `/usr/local/bin` and symlinking `mwcc_compiler` into `tools/` bridged the existing repository layout expectations with a container-level installation.
Building the image in named stages made the Dockerfile readable as a sequence of environment upgrades and toolchain setup phases.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation CI, the Metrowerks compiler, wrapper emulator, exact patching process, and Python tooling are infrastructure dependencies; stabilizing them in a versioned image can be more maintainable than reassembling them inside each workflow.
When moving compiler assets into a container, keep repository scripts that assume `tools/mwcc_compiler` in mind; either preserve that path with symlinks or update scripts consistently.

CI changes that affect matching builds should continue to run both map-generation and nonmatching build configurations; this workflow preserved the matrix entries `GENERATE_MAP=1` and `NON_MATCHING=1`.
If documentation generation uses project Python tooling such as `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py`, the docs workflow also needs the same controlled Python dependency story as the build workflow.

A custom image owned by an individual account may be useful for experimentation, but project CI likely needs an image controlled by the organization; the PR body explicitly noted that doldecomp owners could create their own container.
Because this PR was superseded, future work should consult #751 before reusing this exact Dockerfile or image tag.

naming_conventions.meaning: Proposed custom Docker image tag used by both build and publish workflows.
Environment variable set to `/usr/local/bin/wibo` so make can run MWCC through WiBo.
Directory name expected by existing Melee tooling for the downloaded GC compiler package.
Container-installed shell wrapper that invokes `/usr/local/bin/mwcc_compiler/1.2.5e/mwcceppc.exe` through `$WINE`.
Dockerfile stage names describing successive setup phases for CI tooling.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR did not change game C/assembly matching logic directly.
The build workflow retained the existing matching-related CI matrix modes, including `GENERATE_MAP=1` for producing `GALE01.map` and `NON_MATCHING=1` for nonmatching builds.
The Dockerfile preserved MWLD patch validation with prepatch and postpatch SHA1 checks, which is important for deterministic toolchain behavior.
Installing a wrapper for `mwcceppc` through WiBo is a CI tactic for running the Windows Metrowerks compiler under Linux without rebuilding WiBo per job.

searchable_terms: Use custom container for GitHub Actions
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #751 to see the final or replacement approach for custom GitHub Actions containers.
Check whether the project later moved the Docker image from `ribbanya/melee` to an organization-owned image.
Search current workflows for `container:` and `WINE=$WINE` to see whether this workflow simplification survived.
Search current Dockerfiles or CI images for `mwcc_compiler`, `mwcceppc`, and `mwld_patch.py` to confirm the adopted toolchain layout.
Compare later CI changes for Python 3.11, Doxygen, and `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` handling.
